name: VGG16
fullName: Very Deep Convolutional Networks for Large-Scale Image Recognition
description: VGG16 is a classic CNN architecture characterized by its homogeneous stacks of 3x3 convolutions and 2x2 max pooling. Its simple, linear design demonstrates how stacking small convolutions can build powerful deep visual descriptors.

educationalNote.summary: Retinal layer receiving raw RGB pixel matrices.
educationalNote.detailed: Accepts normalized three-channel RGB images of size 224x224. This spatial standardization ensures weight compatibility in subsequent fully connected layers.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Establishes spatial dimensions for the model.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Fixed input sizes are critical to prevent spatial alignment errors.

calculationSteps.label: Kernel Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: 64 kernels of size 3x3 convolving over 3 channels.

calculationSteps.label: Biases
calculationSteps.explanation: One learnable bias parameter per filter.

calculationSteps.label: Total Parameters
calculationSteps.explanation: Sum of weights and biases.

educationalNote.detailed: Slides 64 filters across input features to extract local patterns (like curves and edges).

calculationSteps.label: Kernel Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: 64 kernels of size 3x3 convolving over 64 channels.

calculationSteps.label: Biases
calculationSteps.explanation: One learnable bias parameter per filter.

calculationSteps.label: Total Parameters
calculationSteps.explanation: Sum of weights and biases.

educationalNote.detailed: Slides 64 filters across input features to extract local patterns (like curves and edges).

educationalNote.summary: Halves spatial resolution while retaining peak features.
educationalNote.detailed: Slides a 2x2 window with stride 2 over each feature map, outputting the maximum value in that cell. Downsamples resolution, decreasing compute while preserving dominant signals.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Pooling achieves downsampling and feature invariance with zero learnable parameters.

educationalNote.summary: Halves spatial resolution while retaining peak features.
educationalNote.detailed: Slides a 2x2 window with stride 2 over each feature map, outputting the maximum value in that cell. Downsamples resolution, decreasing compute while preserving dominant signals.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Pooling achieves downsampling and feature invariance with zero learnable parameters.

educationalNote.summary: Unrolls spatial features into a 1D vector.
educationalNote.detailed: Converts the 3D tensor of shape 7x7x512 into a single vector of length 25088. This step transitions the model from spatial feature mapping to multi-class classification reasoning.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Bridges local convolutions and dense classifiers.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Flattening discards spatial dimensions while preserving the raw activation vector.

calculationSteps.label: Fully Connected Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: Every input element is connected to all 4096 output units via a learnable weight.

calculationSteps.label: Biases
calculationSteps.explanation: One bias term per output unit.

calculationSteps.label: Total Parameters
calculationSteps.explanation: Combined weights and biases.

educationalNote.detailed: This single layer accounts for over 70% of VGG's total parameter count. It performs global visual reasoning across the entire set of flattened features, determining how parts relate to classes.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: The massive parameter footprint of this transition is why modern models prefer Global Average Pooling over Flattening.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Fully connected layers contain the majority of standard CNN parameters.

calculationSteps.label: Fully Connected Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: Every unit from FC1 is connected to all 4096 units of FC2.

calculationSteps.label: Biases
calculationSteps.explanation: One bias term per output unit.

calculationSteps.label: Total Parameters
calculationSteps.explanation: Combined weights and biases.

educationalNote.summary: Second dense layer representing high-level semantic abstractions.
educationalNote.detailed: Performs further non-linear mapping on the 4096-dimensional global visual representations, building high-level classification templates.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Deep fully connected layers learn abstract visual classification features.

calculationSteps.label: Fully Connected Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: Every unit from FC2 is connected to the 1000 final output class units.

calculationSteps.label: Biases
calculationSteps.explanation: One bias term per classification class.

calculationSteps.label: Total Parameters
calculationSteps.explanation: Combined weights and biases.

educationalNote.detailed: The final network stage. Converts learned features into a probability distribution over 1000 ImageNet categories.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Provides interpretable confidence scores representing the model's decisions.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Softmax maps outputs to a valid probability distribution that sums to 1.

groups.name: Classifier
groups.description: Flatten and fully connected classification head
